title: Robotic Telescope Night Plan
seed_problem: value-collecting tour of sky targets under a motion-accumulated pointing-drift accuracy penalty
mutation: composite: couple a value-weighted observation tour to a motion-proportional pointing drift and a limited pool of time-costed recalibrations, so accuracy becomes a currency the tour itself creates and spends; routing and reset placement must be co-designed rather than solving a shortest tour then inserting resets.
strategies: trivial: monotone coordinate sweep, no recalibration (reproduces the checker baseline)
greedy: shortest-tour sweep with recalibrations spread UNIFORMLY (spends the drift budget evenly, wastes it on cheap stretches, and reaches the far jackpot clusters at collapsed accuracy)
value-rush: go straight to the biggest cluster first -> arrives with drift = k*(huge distance), accuracy collapses

insight (strong): keep the near-monotone sweep so each step is short, then recalibrate RIGHT BEFORE the highest-value clusters (choosing which clusters to reset for, since C>K) so their large value is observed at near-zero drift
